en_definition: to form or join a line while waiting for something
example1: We had to queue up for almost twenty minutes.
example1_ru: Нам пришлось встать в очередь почти на двадцать минут.
example2: Students queued up outside the exam hall.

en_definition: to stand in a row of people waiting for a turn
example1: We waited in line at the canteen for lunch.
example1_ru: Мы стояли в очереди в столовой на обед.
example2: He hates waiting in line at the post office.

en_definition: to unfairly join a line ahead of people already waiting
example1: Someone tried to cut in line at the ticket counter.
example1_ru: Кто-то попытался влезть без очереди в кассе.
example2: It's rude to cut in line at the cafeteria.

en_definition: to get a ticket with a number showing your place in line
example1: Take a number and wait until it's called.
example1_ru: Возьмите талон и ждите, пока его не назовут.
example2: We took a number at the bank and sat down.

en_definition: a long flat surface where staff serve customers
example1: Ask at the information counter for directions.
example1_ru: Спросите на информационной стойке дорогу.
example2: She left her bag on the kitchen counter.

en_definition: a short trip to do a small task like shopping
example1: I need to run an errand before class starts.
example1_ru: Мне нужно сделать одно дело перед началом занятий.
example2: She ran several errands on her way home.

en_definition: the usual things you do every day in the same order
example1: My daily routine starts with a short jog.
example1_ru: Моя ежедневная рутина начинается с короткой пробежки.
example2: Moving abroad changed her whole daily routine.

en_definition: a job that needs to be done regularly at home
example1: Cleaning the kitchen is a daily household task.
example1_ru: Уборка кухни — ежедневное домашнее дело.
example2: We share household tasks with our roommates.

en_definition: to carry rubbish outside to be collected
example1: It's your turn to take out the trash tonight.
example1_ru: Сегодня твоя очередь выносить мусор.
example2: He forgot to take out the trash again.

en_definition: to treat waste so it can be used again
example1: We recycle paper and plastic in our building.
example1_ru: Мы перерабатываем бумагу и пластик в нашем доме.
example2: Remember to recycle the empty bottles.

en_definition: a container used for holding rubbish
example1: Put the empty cans in the recycling bin.
example1_ru: Положи пустые банки в мусорное ведро для переработки.
example2: The bin outside our door was full again.

en_definition: to clean a floor using a broom
example1: Could you sweep the kitchen floor, please?
example1_ru: Не мог бы ты подмести пол на кухне, пожалуйста?
example2: He swept the leaves off the balcony.

en_definition: to clean a surface by rubbing it with a cloth
example1: Please wipe the table after you eat.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, вытри стол после еды.
example2: She wiped the window with an old newspaper.

en_definition: neat and in good order
example1: Keep your desk tidy before the inspection.
example1_ru: Держи стол аккуратным перед проверкой.
example2: Her room is always tidy and clean.

en_definition: untidy or in a state of disorder
example1: His desk was messy with papers everywhere.
example1_ru: Его стол был неопрятным, с бумагами повсюду.
example2: The kitchen looked messy after the party.

en_definition: a person who lives near you
example1: Our neighbor is very friendly and helpful.
example1_ru: Наш сосед очень дружелюбный и отзывчивый.
example2: The neighbors complained about the loud music.

en_definition: making a lot of unwanted sound
example1: The street below our window is quite noisy.
example1_ru: Улица под нашим окном довольно шумная.
example2: It's hard to study in such a noisy room.

en_definition: making very little noise; calm
example1: The library is the quietest place on campus.
example1_ru: Библиотека — самое тихое место в кампусе.
example2: We found a quiet spot to study together.

en_definition: a current of cool air moving through a room
example1: Close the window; there's a cold draft.
example1_ru: Закрой окно, там холодный сквозняк.
example2: The draft from the door made the room chilly.

en_definition: having air that feels warm and lacks fresh circulation
example1: The classroom felt stuffy without any open windows.
example1_ru: В классе было душно без открытых окон.
example2: Open a window; it's too stuffy in here.

en_definition: covered with a thin layer of fog
example1: The valley looked misty in the early morning.
example1_ru: Долина выглядела туманной ранним утром.
example2: It was too misty to see the mountains clearly.

en_definition: slightly wet in an unpleasant way
example1: The walls felt damp after all the rain.
example1_ru: Стены были сырыми после всего этого дождя.
example2: Hang your damp towel outside to dry.

en_definition: raining lightly and gently
example1: It was a drizzly morning, so we took umbrellas.
example1_ru: Утро было моросящим, поэтому мы взяли зонты.
example2: Drizzly weather made the streets slippery.

en_definition: to make liquid move suddenly and scatter
example1: The car splashed water onto the sidewalk.
example1_ru: Машина обрызгала тротуар водой.
example2: Children love to splash in puddles after rain.

en_definition: a sky without clouds
example1: We enjoyed a clear sky on our hike.
example1_ru: Мы наслаждались ясным небом во время похода.
example2: The forecast promised a clear sky tomorrow.

en_definition: the loud sound that comes after lightning
example1: The thunder woke us up in the middle of the night.
example1_ru: Гром разбудил нас посреди ночи.
example2: We heard thunder in the distance during dinner.

en_definition: a flash of light in the sky during a storm
example1: A lightning bolt lit up the whole sky.
example1_ru: Молния осветила всё небо.
example2: The lightning bolt struck a tree near the park.

en_definition: a small pool of water on the ground after rain
example1: He jumped over a puddle on the way to class.
example1_ru: Он перепрыгнул через лужу по дороге на занятия.
example2: There were puddles all over the sidewalk.

en_definition: a device used to protect yourself from rain
example1: Don't forget your umbrella; it might rain today.
example1_ru: Не забудь зонт, сегодня может пойти дождь.
example2: She left her umbrella on the bus again.

en_definition: a waterproof coat worn to stay dry in the rain
example1: He wore a raincoat instead of carrying an umbrella.
example1_ru: Он надел дождевик вместо того, чтобы нести зонт.
example2: The raincoat kept her dry during the storm.

en_definition: a cream used to protect skin from the sun
example1: Apply sunscreen before you go to the beach.
example1_ru: Нанеси солнцезащитный крем перед походом на пляж.
example2: She forgot sunscreen and got sunburned.

en_definition: red, sore skin caused by too much sun
example1: He got a bad sunburn on his first day at the beach.
example1_ru: У него был сильный солнечный ожог в первый день на пляже.
example2: Cool water helps soothe a sunburn.

en_definition: a sudden strong movement of air
example1: A gust of wind blew the papers off the desk.
example1_ru: Порыв ветра сдул бумаги со стола.
example2: Hold onto your hat; there's a strong gust of wind.

en_definition: a soft, calm movement of air
example1: A gentle wind moved through the trees.
example1_ru: Лёгкий ветер прошёл сквозь деревья.
example2: We felt a gentle wind on our walk by the river.

en_definition: a broadcast or bulletin that describes coming weather conditions
example1: The weather report predicted snow this weekend.
example1_ru: Прогноз погоды предсказывал снег в эти выходные.
example2: We watched the weather report before packing.

en_definition: a phone app that shows the current and future weather
example1: I checked the weather app before leaving home.
example1_ru: Я проверил приложение погоды перед выходом из дома.
example2: The weather app said it would rain later today.

en_definition: a unit used to measure temperature
example1: It's about twenty degrees Celsius outside today.
example1_ru: Сегодня на улице около двадцати градусов Цельсия.
example2: The water boils at one hundred degrees Celsius.

en_definition: extremely cold, often near or below zero
example1: It was freezing outside, so we stayed indoors.
example1_ru: На улице было морозно, поэтому мы остались дома.
example2: My hands felt freezing after the walk.

en_definition: not extreme; pleasantly warm or cool
example1: The weather here is mild all year round.
example1_ru: Погода здесь мягкая круглый год.
example2: We had a mild winter this year.

en_definition: a period of the year with little or no rain
example1: The dry season here lasts from November to March.
example1_ru: Сухой сезон здесь длится с ноября по март.
example2: Farmers plan carefully around the dry season.

en_definition: a period of the year with frequent heavy rain
example1: Streets often flood during the wet season.
example1_ru: Улицы часто затапливает во время сезона дождей.
example2: Bring an umbrella; it's the wet season now.

en_definition: to find the answer to a problem
example1: We spent an hour trying to solve the puzzle.
example1_ru: Мы потратили час, пытаясь решить головоломку.
example2: She solved the equation in a few minutes.

en_definition: to calculate numbers, often informally
example1: Let me do the math before we split the bill.
example1_ru: Дай мне сделать вычисления перед тем, как мы разделим счёт.
example2: She did the math quickly in her head.

en_definition: to find the total number of something
example1: Count the number of students in the room.
example1_ru: Посчитай количество студентов в комнате.
example2: He counted the coins on the table twice.

en_definition: to calculate the total of several numbers
example1: Add up these numbers to get the final score.
example1_ru: Сложи эти числа, чтобы получить итоговый балл.
example2: The cashier added up all the items quickly.

en_definition: to take one number away from another
example1: Subtract ten from the total amount.
example1_ru: Вычти десять из общей суммы.
example2: She subtracted the discount from the price.

en_definition: to add several amounts together to find the whole sum
example1: Total up the receipts before submitting the expense report.
example1_ru: Суммируй чеки перед подачей отчёта о расходах.
example2: The clerk totaled up our items at the register.

en_definition: to divide something into equal parts among people
example1: We decided to share the bill equally.
example1_ru: Мы решили разделить счёт поровну.
example2: The cake was shared equally among six friends.

en_definition: to change a number to a slightly higher, simpler value
example1: Round up the price to the nearest dollar.
example1_ru: Округли цену до ближайшего доллара.
example2: We rounded up the total for convenience.

en_definition: to guess an approximate value without exact calculation
example1: Try to estimate roughly how long the project will take.
example1_ru: Попробуй приблизительно оценить, сколько займёт проект.
example2: He estimated roughly how much paint they needed.

en_definition: to put information onto paper
example1: Write down the homework before you leave class.
example1_ru: Запиши домашнее задание перед уходом с занятия.
example2: She wrote down every important date in her planner.

en_definition: to draw a line under a word to show it is important
example1: Underline the main idea in each paragraph.
example1_ru: Подчеркни главную мысль в каждом абзаце.
example2: He underlined the deadline in red pen.

en_definition: to draw a line around something to mark it
example1: Circle the correct answer on the test sheet.
example1_ru: Обведи правильный ответ на листе теста.
example2: She circled the important dates on the calendar.

en_definition: to draw a line through something to remove it
example1: Cross out the wrong answer and write the correct one.
example1_ru: Зачеркни неправильный ответ и напиши правильный.
example2: He crossed out the mistake and continued writing.

en_definition: to write the missing information in a form or gap
example1: Fill in the blanks with the correct words.
example1_ru: Заполни пропуски правильными словами.
example2: Please fill in your name and address here.

en_definition: to mark a box with a small check symbol
example1: Tick the box that matches your answer.
example1_ru: Поставь галочку в клетке, которая соответствует твоему ответу.
example2: She ticked all the items on her shopping list.

en_definition: to connect items that belong together or are similar
example1: Match up each word with its correct definition.
example1_ru: Сопоставь каждое слово с его правильным определением.
example2: The exercise asked us to match up pictures and names.

en_definition: to check something quickly before using or submitting it
example1: Look over your essay one more time before class.
example1_ru: Просмотри своё эссе ещё раз перед занятием.
example2: He looked over the contract before signing.

en_definition: to review or discuss something carefully
example1: Let's go over the main points before the test.
example1_ru: Давай разберём основные пункты перед тестом.
example2: The teacher went over the homework answers together with us.

en_definition: to find a solution to a problem by thinking or calculating
example1: I couldn't work out the answer to question five.
example1_ru: Я не смог разобраться с ответом на пятый вопрос.
example2: She worked out a plan to finish the project on time.

en_definition: to understand or solve something after thinking about it
example1: It took a while to figure out the new app.
example1_ru: Понадобилось время, чтобы разобраться с новым приложением.
example2: We finally figured out why the printer wasn't working.

en_definition: to show something by extending your finger toward it
example1: She pointed at the map to show our route.
example1_ru: Она указала на карту, чтобы показать наш маршрут.
example2: He pointed at the mistake in my report.

en_definition: to guide someone through a new place, pointing out important spots
example1: My roommate showed me around the campus on the first day.
example1_ru: Мой сосед по комнате показал мне кампус в первый день.
example2: We showed the new students around the library.

en_definition: to become comfortable in a new place or situation
example1: It took her a few weeks to settle in after moving.
example1_ru: Ей потребовалось несколько недель, чтобы освоиться после переезда.
example2: New students usually settle in by the second month.

en_definition: to become used to a new situation or change something slightly
example1: It took time to adjust to the new time zone.
example1_ru: Понадобилось время, чтобы приспособиться к новому часовому поясу.
example2: He adjusted the chair before sitting down.

en_definition: the feeling of confusion when experiencing a very different culture
example1: She felt culture shock during her first week in China.
example1_ru: Она испытала культурный шок в первую неделю в Китае.
example2: Culture shock usually fades after a few months abroad.

en_definition: a traditional way of doing things in a particular place
example1: It's polite to learn the local customs before you travel.
example1_ru: Вежливо изучить местные обычаи перед поездкой.
example2: Bowing is a local custom in many Asian countries.

en_definition: the accepted rules of polite behavior in a society
example1: Table etiquette can be different from country to country.
example1_ru: Этикет за столом может отличаться от страны к стране.
example2: He studied basic etiquette before his business trip.

en_definition: a custom or belief passed down through generations
example1: Every family has its own holiday traditions.
example1_ru: У каждой семьи свои праздничные традиции.
example2: This festival is an old tradition in the region.

en_definition: to do something enjoyable to mark a special event
example1: We celebrated the end of exams with a big dinner.
example1_ru: Мы отпраздновали окончание экзаменов большим ужином.
example2: They celebrated her birthday at a small restaurant.

en_definition: an official day off work or school for everyone
example1: Classes are cancelled because of the public holiday.
example1_ru: Занятия отменены из-за государственного праздника.
example2: Shops close early on public holidays here.

en_definition: an event or day organized to celebrate something special
example1: The city hosts a lantern festival every autumn.
example1_ru: Город проводит фестиваль фонарей каждую осень.
example2: We visited a food festival near the campus.

en_definition: a shop that sells small gifts related to a place
example1: We bought postcards at the souvenir shop.
example1_ru: Мы купили открытки в магазине сувениров.
example2: The souvenir shop near the temple was very busy.

en_definition: a card with a picture that people send by mail
example1: She sent a postcard to her grandmother from Beijing.
example1_ru: Она отправила открытку своей бабушке из Пекина.
example2: He collects postcards from every city he visits.

en_definition: a shop that sells presents and small items
example1: The museum has a gift shop near the exit.
example1_ru: У музея есть сувенирный магазин рядом с выходом.
example2: We bought a small gift for our host family at the gift shop.

en_definition: a family that welcomes a foreign student or visitor into their home
example1: My host family taught me a lot about local food.
example1_ru: Моя принимающая семья научила меня многому о местной еде.
example2: We stayed with a host family during the exchange program.

en_definition: a bedroom in a student residence building
example1: Our dorm room has two beds and one desk.
example1_ru: В нашей комнате в общежитии две кровати и один стол.
example2: She decorated her dorm room with photos.

en_definition: a room used by more than one person
example1: We live in a shared room with two beds.
example1_ru: Мы живём в общей комнате с двумя кроватями.
example2: A shared room is cheaper than a private one.

en_definition: a room meant for only one person
example1: I paid extra for a single room this year.
example1_ru: Я доплатил за одноместную комнату в этом году.
example2: The single room was quiet but small.

en_definition: a rule about the time you must return home or to a dorm
example1: The dormitory has an eleven o'clock curfew.
example1_ru: В общежитии комендантский час в одиннадцать.
example2: Students must respect the curfew on weekdays.

en_definition: a student who helps manage a dormitory floor
example1: Our resident advisor organized a welcome party.
example1_ru: Наш старший по общежитию организовал приветственную вечеринку.
example2: Ask the resident advisor about the laundry rules.

en_definition: a shared space in a dormitory used for relaxing or studying
example1: We watch movies together in the common room.
example1_ru: Мы вместе смотрим фильмы в общей комнате.
example2: The common room has a small kitchen and sofas.

en_definition: a place where students or workers can buy meals
example1: Lunch in the cafeteria is cheaper than in town.
example1_ru: Обед в столовой дешевле, чем в городе.
example2: The cafeteria closes at eight in the evening.

en_definition: a system where students pay in advance for meals on campus
example1: Most students buy a meal plan for the semester.
example1_ru: Большинство студентов покупают план питания на семестр.
example2: My meal plan covers breakfast and dinner.

en_definition: a machine that sells snacks or drinks for coins or cards
example1: I bought a snack from the vending machine.
example1_ru: Я купил перекус в торговом автомате.
example2: The vending machine near the library only takes cards.

en_definition: a device that provides drinking water in public places
example1: Fill your bottle at the water fountain in the hallway.
example1_ru: Наполни бутылку у питьевого фонтанчика в коридоре.
example2: There's a water fountain outside the gym.

en_definition: a small locked cupboard for storing personal items
example1: Keep your books in the locker near the entrance.
example1_ru: Держи книги в шкафчике возле входа.
example2: She forgot the code to her locker.

en_definition: a person whose job is to protect a building and people in it
example1: The security guard checked our bags at the entrance.
example1_ru: Охранник проверил наши сумки на входе.
example2: A security guard patrols the campus at night.

en_definition: a board where announcements and notices are posted
example1: Check the noticeboard for updates about the trip.
example1_ru: Посмотри на доске объявлений обновления насчёт поездки.
example2: A new schedule was pinned to the noticeboard.

en_definition: a public statement giving information about something
example1: There was an announcement about the exam schedule.
example1_ru: Было объявление насчёт расписания экзаменов.
example2: The announcement said the library would close early.

en_definition: a group of people who meet regularly because of a shared interest
example1: I joined the photography club this semester.
example1_ru: Я вступил в фотоклуб в этом семестре.
example2: The chess club meets every Wednesday afternoon.

en_definition: an organized group of students with a common interest
example1: The debate society holds weekly meetings.
example1_ru: Дискуссионное общество проводит еженедельные встречи.
example2: She became president of the language society.

en_definition: to officially put your name down to join something
example1: You can sign up for the club on the noticeboard.
example1_ru: Ты можешь записаться в клуб на доске объявлений.
example2: We signed up for the volunteer program together.

en_definition: a person who does unpaid work to help others
example1: She works as a volunteer at the community center.
example1_ru: Она работает волонтёром в общественном центре.
example2: The event needed twenty volunteers to run smoothly.

en_definition: a job that involves working fewer hours than a full-time job
example1: He has a part-time job at the campus bookstore.
example1_ru: У него подработка в кампусном книжном магазине.
example2: Many students find a part-time job during summer.

en_definition: a set period of time during which someone works
example1: She works the morning shift at the coffee shop.
example1_ru: Она работает в утреннюю смену в кофейне.
example2: My shift starts at nine and ends at five.

en_definition: the day when workers receive their salary
example1: Payday is always on the last Friday of the month.
example1_ru: День зарплаты всегда в последнюю пятницу месяца.
example2: I'll pay you back on payday.

en_definition: an extra amount of money given for good service
example1: A gratuity is not required, but it's appreciated.
example1_ru: Чаевые не обязательны, но приветствуются.
example2: We left a small gratuity for the waiter.

en_definition: an extra fee added to a bill for service
example1: The restaurant added a ten percent service charge.
example1_ru: Ресторан добавил десять процентов платы за обслуживание.
example2: Check whether the service charge is already included.

en_definition: a machine that lets customers scan and pay for items themselves
example1: We used the self-checkout to save time.
example1_ru: Мы воспользовались кассой самообслуживания, чтобы сэкономить время.
example2: The self-checkout machine rejected my old coin.
